headline: War begins — US-Israeli strikes across 24 Iranian provinces
detail: Coordinated US and Israeli military operations launch simultaneous strikes across all 24 Iranian provinces, targeting IRGC installations, air defenses, and command infrastructure. CENTCOM confirms operations underway.

headline: Ayatollah Khamenei killed in strike
detail: Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei confirmed killed in the initial wave of strikes. Trump later said 'I got him before he got me,' referencing 2024 assassination plots.

headline: Minab school struck — children killed near IRGC barracks
detail: Shajarah Tayyebeh school in Minab hit, adjacent to IRGC barracks. Initial reports of dozens of children killed. Toll eventually rises to 175. Malala Yousafzai: 'They were girls who went to school to learn.'

headline: Iran begins retaliatory missile and drone launches
detail: IRGC launches first wave of retaliatory strikes targeting US bases in the Gulf, UAE, and Israel. Sirens sound across the region. Initial wave includes ballistic missiles and Shahed drones.
source: AFP, Times of Israel, Al Jazeera

headline: Strait of Hormuz warnings issued
detail: IRGC issues warnings regarding Strait of Hormuz navigation. Contradictory statements from military and civilian leadership signal internal power struggle over closure decision.

headline: Global markets brace — oil futures surge in after-hours trading
detail: Brent crude jumps to $73.20 (+3.42%) in Friday close. Chinese off-market trading already approaching $80. Nikkei futures indicate ~1,000 point drop at Monday open.

headline: 6 US service members killed at Camp Shuaiba, Kuwait
detail: Iranian missile strike on Camp Shuaiba kills 6 US service members and seriously wounds 10. First US combat fatalities of the conflict. Kuwait airport also physically damaged by drones.

headline: Iran's MOIS contacts CIA via back-channel
detail: Iran's intelligence ministry (MOIS) makes contact with CIA through intermediaries. First confirmed communication since hostilities began. Substance of contact unclear.

headline: Hezbollah enters the war — rockets into northern Israel
detail: Hezbollah launches multiple rocket barrages into Haifa and Upper Galilee. Naim Qassem: 'We will undertake our duty of confronting the aggression.' IDF responds with strikes on Dahiyeh.

headline: RAF Akrotiri (Cyprus) targeted — UK enters conflict
detail: Iranian missiles target RAF Akrotiri in Cyprus. Two missiles fired toward Cyprus intercepted. PM Starmer grants US use of Diego Garcia and RAF Fairford. RAF Typhoon shoots down Iranian drone over Qatar.

headline: US approval at 27% — Reuters/Ipsos poll
detail: Only 27% of Americans approve of strikes. 56% say Trump too willing to use force. Lowest approval for a US military operation at launch since polling began.

headline: Gandhi Hospital Tehran struck by Israeli bombing
detail: Gandhi Hospital in northern Tehran badly damaged. Patients evacuated. NYT verified video showing significant damage. CENTCOM: 'We are aware of reports concerning civilian harm.'

headline: Ahmadinejad confirmed dead (DISPUTED)
detail: Reports emerge that former President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad was killed in strikes. Status remains disputed with no independent verification. Iranian state media has not confirmed.

headline: CENTCOM: 1,000+ Iranian targets struck in two days
detail: CENTCOM announces aggregate of 1,000+ targets struck including ships, submarines, missile sites, communications, and IRGC C2 centers. 7 military commanders confirmed dead by Tasnim.

headline: All UAE airports closed — Dubai, Abu Dhabi struck
detail: DXB, DWC, AUH, Sharjah all suspended. Dubai International sustained physical damage. Abu Dhabi: 1 killed, 7 injured in drone strike. Burj Khalifa evacuated. 3,400+ flights cancelled.
source: US Embassy UAE, CBS, Al Watan Press

headline: Larijani denies all talks — 'We will not negotiate with the US'
detail: SNSC Secretary Larijani posts on X: 'We will not negotiate with the United States.' Directly contradicts Trump's 'they want to talk' claim. Signals hardline consolidation.

headline: Trump admits succession candidates 'knocked out' by strikes
detail: Trump tells ABC's Jonathan Karl: 'The attack was so successful it knocked out most of the candidates.' US had identified possible Khamenei successors who were killed in initial strikes.

headline: Lebanon front explodes — 31 killed, IDF orders 55 village evacuations
detail: Israeli strikes on Lebanon kill 31, wound 149. IDF evacuation orders for 55 villages. Hezbollah conducts 13 attacks on Israel in 24 hours. Multiple rocket barrages into Haifa.

headline: 3 F-15Es crash in Kuwait — friendly fire incident
detail: Three F-15E Strike Eagles lost in Kuwait in what is determined to be a friendly fire incident. Crews recovered. Investigation underway.

headline: Ras Tanura refinery shut — Saudi oil infrastructure hit
detail: Saudi Arabia's Ras Tanura refinery forced to shut after Iranian drone/missile strikes. Red Crescent reports 555 dead in Iran. Oil markets in turmoil.

headline: Austin TX shooting — 3 dead at federal building
detail: Shooting at federal building in Austin, Texas leaves 3 dead. Suspect Diagne identified. FBI investigating potential connection to war. DHS in Week 3+ of shutdown, CISA ~1/3 staffed.

headline: QatarEnergy halts LNG shipments through Hormuz
detail: QatarEnergy suspends all LNG shipments through the Strait of Hormuz, triggering force majeure concerns. Qatar warns oil could reach $150/bbl in 2-3 weeks if strait stays closed.

headline: IRGC officially declares Strait of Hormuz closed
detail: IRGC formally announces closure of Strait of Hormuz to all shipping. Contradicts earlier FM Araghchi statement that Iran 'does NOT plan to block.' Selective enforcement follows — Chinese ships still passing.

headline: Qatar shoots down Iranian jets approaching airspace
detail: Qatar Air Force intercepts and shoots down Iranian military aircraft approaching Qatari airspace. Significant escalation of Gulf state involvement.

headline: Lebanon bans Hezbollah military activity — PM Salam acts
detail: Lebanese PM Mikati Salam declares Hezbollah military activity 'illegal' and orders the army to act. First Lebanese government move against Hezbollah during active conflict.

headline: US KIA confirmed at 6 — 1,250+ targets struck, 49 IRGC leaders killed
detail: Pentagon confirms 6 US service members killed. CENTCOM updates: 1,250+ targets struck. 49 Iranian military leaders confirmed killed. Rubio admits strikes were preemptive.

headline: Hegseth: 'This is not regime change' — Pentagon undercuts White House
detail: Defense Secretary Hegseth explicitly states 'this is not regime change,' contradicting hawkish White House rhetoric. Trump lays out war objectives focused on denuclearization.

headline: E3 ready to 'destroy' Iran capabilities — Macron nuclear speech
detail: UK, France, and Germany (E3) announce readiness to 'destroy' Iranian military capabilities. Macron delivers speech from nuclear submarine. Iran has <2,000 missiles remaining. Larijani back-channel via Oman.

headline: US Embassy Riyadh struck by drones — Phase 2 imminent
detail: Iranian drones strike near US Embassy compound in Riyadh. 1,500+ IRGC killed. Arab states consider joining coalition. US war approval drops to 25% in polling.

headline: CENTCOM destroys IRGC command & control — simultaneous Tehran/Beirut strikes
detail: CENTCOM confirms destruction of primary IRGC C2 network. IDF conducts simultaneous strikes on Tehran and Beirut. IAEA confirms Natanz nuclear damage. Brent crude at $82.

headline: IRGC threatens Cyprus — missile warning to Akrotiri
detail: IRGC issues explicit threat against Cyprus and RAF Akrotiri base. Bahrain Sitra clashes between protesters and security forces. Regional destabilization accelerating.

headline: E3 formally enters war — European coalition forms
detail: UK, France, and Germany formally commit military assets. Greece, Italy, Portugal, Spain (defensive only), and Australia also deploy. AWS data centers in region hit. Supertanker rates at all-time highs.

headline: Secret nuclear facility destroyed — Parchin struck twice
detail: US strikes destroy a previously undisclosed nuclear facility. Parchin military complex struck twice. Nuclear-specific damage not confirmed but IAEA monitoring disrupted.

headline: Assembly of Experts attacked during successor vote
detail: Strike hits Assembly of Experts meeting during emergency session to vote on Khamenei successor. Mojtaba Khamenei subsequently named as successor despite disruption.

headline: 10+ tankers burning in Strait of Hormuz
detail: Over 10 oil tankers reported on fire in and around the Strait of Hormuz. Combination of Iranian mines, missile strikes, and collateral damage. 150+ tankers anchored outside the strait.

headline: France deploys carrier — Trump threatens Spain
detail: France deploys aircraft carrier Charles de Gaulle to the region. Trump threatens Spain over perceived insufficient support. 3 Western embassies closed in Tehran.

headline: US submarine sinks IRIS Dena — first torpedo kill since WWII
detail: US Navy submarine torpedoes and sinks Iranian frigate IRIS Dena off Sri Lanka. First US submarine torpedo kill of a warship since World War II. Crew fate unknown.

headline: NATO intercepts Iranian missile heading toward Turkey
detail: NATO air defense systems intercept an Iranian ballistic missile on trajectory toward Turkish territory. First NATO collective defense activation since Article 5 invocation after 9/11.

headline: F-35 scores first air-to-air kill in combat
detail: US F-35 Lightning II achieves first confirmed air-to-air kill in combat, downing an Iranian aircraft. Historic first for the fifth-generation fighter program.

headline: MOIS contacts CIA — death toll passes 1,045
detail: Iran's Ministry of Intelligence makes direct contact with CIA. Red Crescent updates death toll to 1,045+. CIA confirmed arming Kurdish forces along Iraqi border.

headline: P&I insurance cancelled for Gulf — KOSPI crashes 12%
detail: Major maritime insurers cancel Protection & Indemnity coverage for all Gulf transits. South Korea's KOSPI index crashes 12% on Hormuz closure fears. Only 2 transits/day through strait.

headline: Iraq plunged into total communications blackout
detail: Iraq experiences complete communications and internet blackout following missile strikes on Baghdad infrastructure. Baghdad Airport area hit by Iranian missiles.

headline: Senate war powers resolution fails 47-53
detail: US Senate votes down war powers resolution to constrain military operations against Iran, 47-53. Constitutional challenge to presidential authority rejected along largely party lines.

headline: IRGC claims hypersonic missile launch against US carrier
detail: IRGC claims successful launch of hypersonic missiles targeting US carrier group. Pentagon does not confirm any hits on carrier assets. Claim assessed as likely propaganda.

headline: IRGC claims US oil tanker hit in Gulf — drones strike Azerbaijan
detail: IRGC claims strike on US-owned oil tanker in the Gulf. Iranian drones cross into Azerbaijan, striking targets. EU issues specific condemnation of attacks on Turkey and Azerbaijan.

headline: Iran death toll reaches 1,230 — Azadi Stadium destroyed
detail: Red Crescent updates Iran death toll to 1,230. Tehran's iconic 100,000-seat Azadi Stadium destroyed in strikes. EU warns of Iranian civil war risk. UNICEF reports children among casualties.

headline: European naval coalition forms — Maersk suspends Gulf bookings
detail: UK, France, Greece, Italy, Portugal, Spain, and Australia form defensive naval coalition in Gulf. Maersk suspends all Gulf shipping bookings. Azerbaijan halts cross-border traffic with Iran.

headline: Trump demands role in choosing Iran's next leader
detail: Trump states 'I have to be involved' in selecting Iran's next leader. Calls Mojtaba Khamenei 'a lightweight.' Later says 'maybe' to a religious leader if pro-US/Israel.

headline: IDF announces 'next phase' — 2,500 strikes, 80% air defense destroyed
detail: IDF declares 'next phase' of operations. 2,500 strikes conducted to date. Claims 80% of Iranian air defenses destroyed. 400+ targets in western Iran on Friday alone.

headline: Massive Dahiya bombardment — 123 dead in Lebanon
detail: IDF conducts 26 strikes on Dahiyeh (Beirut southern suburbs). 123 dead in Lebanon in 24 hours. IRGC Air Force command and Hezbollah naval HQ destroyed. 700,000 displaced by Beirut evacuation order.

headline: House war powers resolution defeated
detail: US House of Representatives votes down war powers resolution following Senate's earlier rejection. Both chambers fail to constrain presidential war authority.

headline: 30+ Iranian ships sunk — BAPCO refinery hit in Bahrain
detail: Pentagon reports 30+ Iranian naval vessels sunk or disabled. BAPCO refinery in Bahrain hit by Iranian strike — fire contained. Hormuz enforcement becomes selective. UK basing in region confirmed.

headline: B-2 bombers hit underground sites — Khamenei bunker destroyed
detail: B-2 Spirit stealth bombers deploy penetrator munitions against deep underground IRGC launch facilities and 'Space Command equivalent.' Khamenei's underground command bunker confirmed destroyed.

headline: Phase 2 operations begin — surge 'dramatically' coming
detail: US and coalition forces officially begin Phase 2 of operations. Hegseth announces surge will increase 'dramatically.' Expanded target sets including deeper infrastructure.

headline: Iran regular Army enters combat — 'will expand attacks'
detail: Iran's regular Army (Artesh) confirmed as sustained combatant alongside IRGC. Army statement: will 'expand attacks in coming days.' 610,000 active forces (350K army + 190K IRGC). Strategy shifts to war of attrition.

headline: US Army acknowledges Minab school — 175 children; Iran dead 1,332
detail: US Army acknowledges responsibility investigation for Minab school strike that killed 175 children. School 'likely' hit due to proximity to IRGC facility. Red Crescent updates total Iranian dead to 1,332.

headline: Pezeshkian signals mediation underway — won't name countries
detail: Iranian President Pezeshkian confirms mediation efforts are underway but refuses to identify mediating countries. Putin calls Pezeshkian to express solidarity.

headline: Trump: 'UNCONDITIONAL SURRENDER' on Truth Social
detail: Trump posts demand for Iran's 'UNCONDITIONAL SURRENDER' on Truth Social. Later clarified via Axios: means total military destruction, not formal capitulation document.

headline: Leavitt: 4-6 week timeline — intel agencies shopping for replacements
detail: White House spokesperson Leavitt gives first official war timeline: 4-6 more weeks. Confirms intel agencies are considering 'a number' of potential Iranian leadership replacements.

headline: Trump/CNN: 25 warships struck, 'destroyed their navy' — quadruple production
detail: Trump tells CNN that 25 Iranian warships have been struck, claiming to have 'destroyed their navy.' Agrees to quadruple US weapons production. CSIS estimates war cost at $3.7B per 100 hours ($891M/day).

headline: Haaretz: Russia providing Iran intelligence on US forces
detail: Haaretz reports Russia is sharing intelligence with Iran about US military positions and movements. If confirmed, would constitute near co-belligerency. Kremlin positioning as energy beneficiary.

headline: Kurdish leaders confirm US/Israel coordination — ground op 'within a week'
detail: Kurdish leaders publicly confirm year-long coordination with US and Israel to Reuters and Al Jazeera. 5,000-6,000 fighters positioned on Iraqi border. Ground operation 'very likely within a week.' CIA 'still active and strong.'

headline: Iran strikes oil tanker off Kuwait — Mehrabad airbase hit
detail: Iran strikes 'US-owned' oil tanker off Kuwait, which catches fire. New escalation into commercial shipping warfare. IDF/USAF strikes Mehrabad airbase in Tehran. Shiraz reports 20 dead from strikes.

headline: IDF displacement order for Qom — Tehran school #4 struck
detail: IDF issues unprecedented displacement order for the holy city of Qom — first inside Iran. Tehran elementary school (#4) struck. Golestan Palace (UNESCO World Heritage Site) hit. 4 schools total damaged.

headline: WTI hits $92 — Qatar airspace partially reopens
detail: WTI crude surges to $92 (from ~$79-81). Brent at $87. Qatar warns $150/bbl in 2-3 weeks if Hormuz stays closed. All Gulf force majeure declarations expected 'in next few days.' Qatar airspace partially reopened.

headline: USS Gerald R. Ford enters Red Sea — Baghdad Airport attacked
detail: USS Gerald R. Ford carrier strike group enters Red Sea via Suez Canal. Baghdad Airport area struck by Iranian missiles. Jordan actively shooting down Iranian drones over Irbid — new defensive belligerent.
source: US Navy, Reuters, Jordanian Armed Forces

headline: Germany withdrawing from Bahrain/Kuwait — Merz distances from Trump
detail: Germany announces withdrawal of personnel from Bahrain and Kuwait. Chancellor Merz publicly distances from Trump's approach. Spain's Sánchez calls war an 'error we'll pay for.' Macron condemns 'unacceptable' UNIFIL attack.

headline: Cumulative toll: Iran 1,332 dead; Lebanon 217; 100K-200K Americans stranded
detail: Iran: 1,332 dead, 6,000+ wounded, 181 children (UNICEF). Lebanon: 217 dead, 798 wounded, 95,000 displaced. Israel: 12 dead, 777 hospitalized. US: 6 KIA. Gulf states: multiple casualties. 100K-200K Americans stranded in region; ~20K returned, 10K received State Dept help.
source: Red Crescent, UNICEF, WHO, State Dept

headline: War costs $3.7B per 100 hours — Dow drops 450+
detail: CSIS estimates war cost at $3.7B per 100 hours ($891M/day), with $3.5B unbudgeted. Trump agrees to quadruple weapons production. Dow Jones drops 450+ points. 23,000 flights cancelled cumulative. India granted 30-day oil waiver.

headline: Iran internet blackout enters Day 7 — 'war of attrition' framing
detail: Iran's internet blackout reaches 144+ hours. Dollar at 101,650 toman on parallel market. Persian-language media shifts framing to 'war of attrition — make victory too costly.' Khamenei funeral postponed indefinitely.
